postText: A sanitary sewer system Amazon is constructing for 2 of its ~30 AI data center buildings at Project Rainier in New Carlisle, IN is designed to use more than 1.6 million gallons per day on hot summer days, recent IDEM filings show https://t.co/cuRyNZ41kK
keyFindings: The development plan incorporates an AEP substation and a private substation to support significant electrical power requirements.
The site features six 2-story 'Titus' buildings (1A-1F) with a finished floor elevation (FFE) of 725.5 feet.
Capacity for future expansion is indicated by eight designated building pads labeled 2A-2C and 3A-3E.
A 16.04-acre detention basin is located at the edge of the site within a designated 'Zone A' Special Flood Hazard Area.

outcome.reason: Kernel could not bind an underspecified partial

postText: A sanitary sewer system Amazon is constructing for 2 of its ~30 AI data center buildings at Project Rainier in New Carlisle, IN is designed to use more than 1.6 million gallons per day on hot summer days, recent IDEM filings show https://t.co/cuRyNZ41kK
keyFindings: The data center project includes a current requirement for 6 units and a future expansion of 13 units, each with a design flow of 2,895 gpd/unit.
The total average design flow for the collection system is estimated at 55,000 gallons per day (gpd).
A high peaking factor of 29.3 is applied, resulting in a peak design flow capacity requirement of 1,612,000 gpd.
The design parameters are explicitly tied to the 327 IAC 3-6-11 regulatory requirement for flow rates.

sourceLines.sourceText: Refer to 327 IAC 3-6-11 for Design Flow Rate Requirements
